dublinCore.dc:title: 96 HR 7255 IH: Santini-Burton Modernization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Kiley of California (for himself, Ms. Titus, and Ms. Lee of Nevada) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend Public Law 96–586 to modernize the authority of the Forest Service to acquire and administer land under that Act, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Santini-Burton Modernization Act of 2026.

section.header: Environmentally sensitive land in the Lake Tahoe Basin

subsection.text: Section 1 of Public Law 96–586 (94 Stat. 3381) (commonly known as the Santini-Burton Act) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (6), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (7), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: the Lake Tahoe Basin is the homeland of the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California, but that Tribe owns less than 0.5 percent of the land in the Lake Tahoe Basin, and that limited land ownership and the presence of that land in the Lake Tahoe Basin hamper the ability of the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California to provide access and cultural resources for the members of that Tribe.
in subsection (b), by inserting and management after acquisition.

subsection.text: Section 3 of Public Law 96–586 (94 Stat. 3383; 114 Stat. 2357; 130 Stat. 1790) (commonly known as the Santini-Burton Act) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (a)(3), in the first sentence, by inserting the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California, and after local government agencies,;

clause.text: by striking Land and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), land

clause.text: in subparagraph (A) (as so designated), by striking United States National Forest System; except that the Secretary and inserting the following: “National Forest System.

item.text: by striking lands each place it appears and inserting land;
by striking which are unsuitable and inserting acquired under this section that is unsuitable; and
by inserting or to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California after local government; and

subclause.text: by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: Amounts made available to carry out this section may be used for the administrative costs of transfers of land and interests in land under this subsection.

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds appropriated pursuant to this Act for the purpose of the acquisition of land and interests in land under this section may be used by the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, for—

clause.text: land management activities on land acquired under this section within the Lake Tahoe Basin; and
land management activities on National Forest System land within the boundaries of the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit.

subparagraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Agriculture may transfer funds made available pursuant to this Act for the purpose of the acquisition of land and interests in land under this section to appropriate units of State or local government or to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California to carry out land management activities on land acquired under this section and transferred to that unit of State or local government or to that Tribe.
Land management activities that may be carried out under subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall include activities for the purposes of—

clause.text: maintaining the wildland-urban interface (as defined in section 101 of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 (16 U.S.C. 6511));
preventing and mitigating environmental impacts due to recreational use;
preserving cultural sites and indigenous management practices; and
scientific research to support decisions relating to land management activities described in clauses (i) through (v).

The Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, may enter into partnerships with the heads of applicable Federal agencies to carry out land management activities under subparagraph (A).

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, may enter into partnerships with, and transfer funds appropriated pursuant to this Act for the purpose of the acquisition of land and interests in land under this section to, appropriate units of State or local government, the Tahoe Regional Planning Agency, or the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California to develop and implement the land management activities described in subparagraph (C) and projects to provide public access to—

subclause.text: land in the Lake Tahoe Basin acquired under this section;
land with a nexus to Federal land in the Lake Tahoe Basin or the shoreline of Lake Tahoe; or
land in the Lake Tahoe Basin that is of cultural significance to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California.

clause.text: Not later than March 15 of each fiscal year, the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Forest Supervisor of the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit, shall develop a spending plan for activities under this paragraph for the next fiscal year consistent with the priorities of the Lake Tahoe Environmental Improvement Program.
In developing the spending plan under clause (i), the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Forest Supervisor of the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit, shall consult with—

subclause.text: the States of California and Nevada;
the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California; and

clause.text: The ranking of management activities in the spending plan developed under clause (i) shall be based on—

subclause.text: the potential to significantly contribute to the achievement and maintenance of the environmental threshold carrying capacities adopted by the Tahoe Regional Planning Agency and the Tahoe Regional Planning Compact (Public Law 96–551; 94 Stat. 3233);
the ability to measure progress or success of the management activity;
the ability of the management activity to have multiple benefits;
the ability of the management activity to leverage other contributions;
inclusion on the 5-year priority list for the Lake Tahoe Environmental Improvement Program; and
whether there is stakeholder support for the management activity.

clause.text: Any funds made available under this paragraph and allocated under the spending plan developed under clause (i)—

subclause.text: may be used for administrative costs of carrying out the spending plan; and

subparagraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any funds made available under this paragraph shall supplement, and not supplant, any other amounts available to the Secretary of Agriculture for expenditure in the Lake Tahoe Basin and any other amounts made available by Congress.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any funds transferred under this paragraph to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California, an appropriate unit of State or local government, or the Tahoe Regional Planning Agency shall be considered to be non-Federal matching funds for purposes of any other provision of Federal law.
in subparagraph (A), by striking and at the end;
by redesignating subparagraph (B) as subparagraph (C); and
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following:
Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit means the land area included in the management unit created by the Forest Service in 1973; and

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (g) as subsection (h); and
by inserting after subsection (f) the following:

subsection.header: Acquisition and management of land of cultural significance to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, may transfer funds appropriated pursuant to this Act for the purpose of the acquisition of land and interests in land under this section to the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California to acquire and manage land of cultural significance to that Tribe in the Lake Tahoe Basin for the purposes of preservation, access, and land management.
